Module 0: Getting Started 0.07 | Portfolio Management Overview

Portfolio Management addresses the process of examining individual assets and assembling the best possible combination for prospective investors. Areas of focus include portfolio risk and return, diversification and risk reduction, portfolio planning and construction, individual behavioral biases, and portfolio risk management. 

 

Learning Modules and Lessons

Portfolio Risk and Return: Part I

Lesson 1: Historical Return and Risk

Lesson 2: Other Investment Characteristics

Lesson 3: Risk Aversion and Portfolio Selection

Lesson 4: Utility Theory and Indifference Curves

Lesson 5: Application of Utility Theory to Portfolio Selection

Lesson 6: Portfolio Risk & Portfolio of Two Risky Assets

Lesson 7: Portfolio of Many Risky Assets

Lesson 8: The Power of Diversification

Lesson 9: Efficient Frontier: Investment Opportunity Set & Minimum Variance Portfolios

Lesson 10: Efficient Frontier: A Risk-Free Asset and Many Risky Assets

Lesson 11: Efficient Frontier: Optimal Investor Portfolio

Portfolio Risk and Return: Part II

Lesson 1: Capital Market Theory: Risk-Free and Risky Assets

Lesson 2: Capital Market Theory: The Capital Market Line

Lesson 3: Capital Market Theory: CML - Leveraged Portfolios

Lesson 4: Systematic and Nonsystematic Risk

Lesson 5: Return Generating Models

Lesson 6: Calculation and Interpretation of Beta

Lesson 7: Capital Asset Pricing Model: Assumptions and the Security Market Line

Lesson 8: Capital Asset Pricing Model: Applications

Lesson 9: Beyond CAPM: Limitations and Extensions of CAPM

Lesson 10: Portfolio Performance Appraisal Measures

Lesson 11: Applications of the CAPM in Portfolio Construction

Portfolio Management: An Overview

Lesson 1: Portfolio Perspective: Diversification and Risk Reduction

Lesson 2: Portfolio Perspective: Risk-Return Trade-off, Downside Protection, Modern Portfolio Theory

Lesson 3: Steps in the Portfolio Management Process

Lesson 4: Types of Investors

Lesson 5: The Asset Management Industry

Lesson 6: Pooled Interest - Mutual Funds

Lesson 7: Pooled Interest - Type of Mutual Funds

Lesson 8: Pooled Interest - Other Investment Products

Basics of Portfolio Planning and Construction

Lesson 1: The Investment Policy Statement

Lesson 2: IPS Risk and Return Objectives

Lesson 3: IPS Constraints

Lesson 4: Gathering Client Information

Lesson 5: Portfolio Construction and Capital Market Expectations

Lesson 6: Strategic Asset Allocation

Lesson 7: Portfolio Construction Principles

Lesson 8: ESG Considerations in Portfolio Planning and Construction

The Behavioral Biases of Individuals

Lesson 1: Behavioral Bias Categories

Lesson 2: Cognitive Errors

Lesson 3: Emotional Biases

Lesson 4: Behavioral Finance and Market Behavior

Introduction to Risk Management

Lesson 1: Risk Management Process

Lesson 2: Risk Management Framework

Lesson 3: Risk Governance - An Enterprise View

Lesson 4: Risk Tolerance

Lesson 5: Risk Budgeting

Lesson 6: Identification of Risk - Financial Vs. Non-Financial Risk

Lesson 7: Interactions Between Risks

Lesson 8: Measuring and Modifying Risk: Drivers and Metrics

Lesson 9: Risk Modification: Prevention, Avoidance, and Acceptance

Lesson 10: Risk Modification: Transferring, Shifting, and How to Choose
